items.title: SA primary school principals' association query free public school policy

children.content: A peak body for primary school principals says it is worried abolishing public school fees will see more money flow to wealthier schools, but the SA education minister says all parents were "doing it tough" and federal funding would go to addressing inequality.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a man and a woman looking at each other

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a man and a woman looking at each other

items.title: 'It's tragic': Hundreds to be impacted after Seafood giant folds

children.content: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

image.altText: raptis fleets docked at the karumba wharf

items.title: Free dental clinic changes lives in cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Demand is high for an Adelaide dental program that offers free services to patients who have experienced homelessness or who have trouble accessing conventional care.

image.altText: A woman smiles as she holds a guitar across her lap, sitting in a park

items.title: Could nurse practitioners help ease the pressure on regional health?

children.content: Nurse practitioners are helping ease some of the burden on doctors in rural areas as services look for more options to see patients.

image.altText: A man with brown hair and a full beard smiling

items.title: Woman who appeared 'belligerent' after fatal crash sentenced

children.content: A woman who was found to be mentally incompetent when she caused a fatal multi-car crash has been released into a transitional mental health facility.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair wearing a black tshirt sits in a room at a desk. A green chair is behind her

items.title: Shooters deployed to curb Kangaroo Island's big little corella problem

children.content: Thermal imaging, infrared technology and crack marksmanship are deployed to battle the island's growing little corella population.

gallery.altText: A little corella in a tree with its wings slightly open

thumbnail.altText: A little corella in a tree with its wings slightly open

items.title: MP's extra voting paper for SA upper house among electoral problems under scrutiny

children.content: As authorities commit to a review of problems faced by South Australian voters during this month's election, a former Liberal MP says he was concerned to received an extra ballot paper in the post.

image.altText: A collection of SA ballot papers including two for the Legislative Council.

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: Coroner finds paramedic who made 'man flu' comments failed to meet standards

children.content: A coroner has recommended the South Australian Ambulance Service consider implementing an inclusive language program after an inquest heard an Indian national was told he was overreacting with the "man flu" in the days before he died of chronic pneumonia.

image.altText: Paramedic in green work clothes with grey hair stands next to ambulance

items.title: South Australia wins back-to-back Shield titles with stunning win over Victoria

children.content: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: South Australia bowler Henry Thornton jumps, shouts and punches the air to celebrate a Sheffield Shield wicket.

items.title: Artists and scientists unite to throw spotlight on seaweed's 'beauty'

children.content: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

image.altText: An underwater shot of colourful seaweed.

children.content: Police in Iraq say they are searching for an American journalist who has been kidnapped in Baghdad, possibly by an Iranian-backed militia group.

image.altText: Shelly Kittleson stands in front of a sunset and the site of ruins.

children.content: US companies including Microsoft, Google and Apple could be targeted, while Donald Trump urges the world to buy American fuel.

articles.title: Trump tells allies 'go get your own oil' from Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

children.content: US President Donald Trump says the US will leave Iran "very soon" has given an estimate of "two weeks, maybe three". Follow live.

items.title: Lawsuit filed against Taylor Swift over The Life of a Showgirl album

children.content: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

image.altText: Taylor Swift poses in a body of water in a glittering silver corset

items.title: Mine may be shut down amid money laundering allegations, bikie claims

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

image.altText: An aerial of mining buildings in a forest and huge mountains in the background.

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: 'We are an expert': How Lebanon's airport keeps running despite Israeli strikes

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

items.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes

children.content: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: Spurs fans protest over new manager as post-Postecoglou woes deepen

children.content: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

image.altText: Roberto De Zerbi sits on the bench and looks to his left

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

articles.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss

items.title: Judge halts Trump's White House demolition and ballroom project

children.content: A federal court has ordered the Trump administration to suspend work on a $400 million ballroom on the site of the demolished East Wing of the White House, barring construction from proceeding without congressional approval.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump holds up a picture of a grand ballroom while he sits in the Oval Office

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A man and a woman smile at the camera.

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: Anger at developer's plan to build $10m mansion on illegally cleared land

children.content: A property developer who illegally cut down hundreds of native trees has lodged a new plan to build a lavish $10 million, six-bedroom mansion in Sydney's south.

image.altText: A render of a house.

items.title: Star completes sale of Queen's Wharf stake to Hong Kong partners

children.content: Star Entertainment confirms it has relinquished its stake in the Destination Brisbane Consortium to Hong Kong partners Chow Tai Fook Enterprises and Far East Consortium.

image.altText: A sign that reads "The Star" below a star shape on the side of a high rise building.

items.title: Police allege murder victim's body was set alight after death

children.content: A 37-year-old man has been arrested and charged with murder a week after burning human remains were found at a Dimbulah property.

image.altText: A police officer standing next to a trailer in a backyard.

items.title: Phone ban in classrooms extended to include smartwatches, headphones

children.content: Victorian students in public and private schools will be banned from wearing smartwatches with internet connectivity and using headphones in classrooms from next year after the government announced an extension of the device ban in schools.

image.altText: Close up of a smart watch, as worn by a seven-year-old boy.

items.title: Farmers think outside the box as rising fuel costs add to drought woes

children.content: Farmers are turning to roadside grazing, selling up stock, and, in one case, shipping cattle to an island to navigate the drought and the rising cost of diesel.

image.altText: A young woman in a brown hat smiles to camera, with cattle grazing the roadside in the background.

items.title: NFL accused of being 'sexist institution' by first female official

children.content: One of the first female officials to be hired by the NFL, Robin DeLorenzo, says that she suffered "open hostility" and "gender-based scrutiny" during her three years as an official in the league.

image.altText: Robin DeLorenzo looks up

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

gallery.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

items.title: Less than half of states signed up as national gun reform deadline passes

children.content: The federal government's March deadline for national gun reform has come and gone, with more than half the states and territories still opposed to parts of the plan. Only four have committed to a gun buyback scheme.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wearing glasses and looking stern.

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.
